project.name: LotteryNetPro
project.description: LotteryNetPro is an Android lottery POS project with Kotlin/Jetpack Compose, Supabase-backed tickets/results, Edge Functions, and automation scripts for lottery results. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.name: Android App
layers.description: Kotlin Android application code, app lifecycle, models, repositories, and shared core logic.

layers.name: Compose UI
layers.description: Jetpack Compose and Android UI screens for sales, tickets, monitoring, finance, admin, and cashier flows.

layers.name: Tickets, Sales, Results
layers.description: Business-critical ticket creation, validation, payout, printing, WhatsApp, results, and sales modules.

layers.name: Supabase Server
layers.description: Supabase Edge Functions, database migrations, RPC scripts, and server-side lottery workflows.

layers.name: Automation Scripts
layers.description: Node, Python, PowerShell, and batch scripts used for QA, deployments, migrations, and result scraping.

layers.name: Tests and QA
layers.description: Unit tests, Android tests, and generated QA utilities that verify ticket, sales, and server behavior.

layers.name: Build and Config
layers.description: Gradle, manifest, ProGuard, JSON, YAML, environment templates, and project configuration.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Markdown and text documentation describing setup, fixes, operations, and project notes.

layers.name: Other Project Files
layers.description: Files and symbols that do not fit the primary architectural layers.

tour.title: Project Shape
tour.description: Start with the Android App and Compose UI layers to understand how the POS is organized on device.

tour.title: Ticket Flow
tour.description: Follow the ticket, sales, result, payout, print, and WhatsApp files because these are the highest-risk business flows.

tour.title: Server and Data
tour.description: Inspect Supabase Edge Functions and SQL files to see where validation, persistence, results, and administrative controls live.

tour.title: Verification
tour.description: Use the Tests and QA layer plus automation scripts to find the project checks and operational smoke tests.

tour.title: Build Configuration
tour.description: Review Gradle, manifest, workflow, and deployment configuration to understand releases and runtime wiring.
